_meta.name: Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS)
_meta.what: India's compliance carbon market. Obligated entities in notified energy-intensive sectors are given a greenhouse-gas emission-intensity (GEI) target; those that beat it earn tradable Carbon Credit Certificates, while those that miss it must buy certificates or pay a penalty.
_meta.status_note: This is a readiness check, whether your client's sector is obligated and what to prepare. It is not a CCTS compliance calculation; the GEI baseline, target gap and credit valuation are a separate step.

primary_sources.label: ICAP, Compliance obligations under India's CCTS enter force for seven sectors
ICAP, India notifies emission-intensity targets for nine sectors under the CCTS

chemicals.note: Obligated only if the plant is in one of these notified sub-sectors.

power_and_energy.note: Petroleum refineries are obligated; pure power generators are covered under the PAT/energy regime, not CCTS GEI targets.

textile_and_apparel.note: Textile is one of the nine notified GEI-target sectors.

mechanism: Each obligated entity gets an annual GEI target (tCO2e per unit of output) against an FY2023-24 baseline. Beat the target → earn Carbon Credit Certificates to sell; miss it → buy certificates or pay a penalty. Compliance is verified via an accredited carbon-verification (MRV) process.

timeline.event: GEI targets notified for the first 7 sectors (~490 obligated entities); Iron & Steel and Fertiliser targets to follow (~740 entities once all nine are notified).
First two compliance years, entities must meet their notified GEI targets.
Action plans to BEE, verified MRV data submission, and the first Carbon Credit Certificate trading.

checklist.task: Confirm the entity is a notified obligated unit
checklist.detail: Obligation is at the installation level within a notified sector and above the energy/output threshold, confirm your specific plant appears on the obligated-entity list, not just that the sector is covered.
Compute greenhouse-gas emission intensity (tCO2e per unit of product) for the baseline year using verified energy + production data.
Find the entity's annual target for FY2025-26 and FY2026-27 and the gap to the current GEI, that gap is the surplus (sellable) or shortfall (to be bought/penalised).
Put monitoring, reporting and verification in place so emissions + output data can be third-party verified for the compliance submission.
Decide whether the entity will likely generate or need to buy Carbon Credit Certificates, and factor that into the engagement, this is where the quantification step follows.
